commit.message:
Reject banned inbound peers at HELLO before completing handshake

Move ban check into Peer::accept() handshake, immediately after recv_hello()
and before sending any response. Mirrors stellar-core's Peer::recvHello()
which checks isBanned() before AUTH. The post-handshake check in
handle_accepted_inbound_peer remains as defense-in-depth.

Fixes #1150

* Add MotorController module with IMU-aided driving

New motor_controller.py module providing high-level async motor control:
- Accelerometer-based distance driving (forward_mm/backward_mm) using
  double-integration with proportional deceleration for accurate stopping
- Gyro-based precise turning (turn/turn_left/turn_right) using IMU feedback
- Time-based driving and turning (forward/backward/timed_turn) preserved
- Acceleration ramps for smooth start/stop
- front_face orientation support: rotates accelerometer X/Y axes to match
  actual robot forward direction regardless of HexDrive mounting position
- Diagnostic output ([MC-DIAG]) for calibration and debugging
- run_instructions() translates button sequences respecting drive_mode setting

app.py changes:
- Import and create MotorController when HexDrive is detected
- Wire async instruction execution via _run_instructions_async()
- Add settings: drive_mode (Time/Distance), drive_step_mm, accel_scale

constants.py changes:
- Add drive mode, distance step, and accelerometer tuning constants

Note: Gyro-based turning has not yet been tuned/calibrated to the same
degree as accelerometer distance driving. Distance mode achieves ~98mm
accuracy on 100mm targets after calibration.

commit.message:
fix: use +incdir+ for VIP include resolution on Xcelium

Xcelium resolves `include paths from the working directory (build/),
not relative to the including file. Change all include paths in
spadmic_vip_pkg.sv from '../xxx/' to 'xxx/' (relative to tb/vip/)
and add +incdir+$TOP_ROOT/tb/vip to the xrun args.

commit.message:
refactor: make UniSchedulerBundle polymorphic over Spec type

Introduce HasGenericDispatchSpec typeclass to allow UniSchedulerBundle
to carry a scheduler-specific spec while converting to GenericDispatchSpec
for the generic dispatch machinery:

  Class HasGenericDispatchSpec (Spec : Type) := {
    to_generic_dispatch_spec : Spec -> GenericDispatchSpec
  }.

UniSchedulerBundle now takes (J : JobId -> Prop) (Spec : Type)
with a HasGenericDispatchSpec Spec constraint:
  usb_spec : Spec  (was GenericDispatchSpec)
  usb_refines : dispatcher_refines_policy (to_generic_dispatch_spec usb_spec) usb_policy
  (field renamed from usb_refinement to usb_refines)

Instances:
  HasGenericDispatchSpec_GenericDispatchSpec  (identity, UniSchedulerInterface.v)
  HasGenericDispatchSpec_EDFSchedulerSpec     (edf_generic projection, EDF.v)

EDF now uses EDFSchedulerSpec as Spec, carrying the min-deadline invariant.
FIFO continues to use GenericDispatchSpec via the identity instance.
UniSchedulerLemmas updated to thread through to_generic_dispatch_spec.
SchedulableExamples updated with explicit Spec type arguments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reject unsolicited tx sets and refresh cache on access

Only cache tx sets that were actually pending (requested) — unsolicited
tx sets from the network are now rejected by receive() to prevent cache
poisoning. Additionally, get() now refreshes cached_at so actively-used
entries survive eviction under cache pressure.

Fixes #1179

commit.message:
feat: introduce UniSchedulerBundle with EDF/FIFO instances

Add UniSchedulerInterface.v and UniSchedulerLemmas.v to define the
standard single-CPU scheduler bundle interface:

  UniSchedulerBundle (J : JobId -> Prop) : Type
    usb_candidates    : CandidateSource
    usb_candidates_ok : CandidateSourceSpec J usb_candidates
    usb_spec          : GenericDispatchSpec
    usb_policy        : DispatchPolicy
    usb_policy_sane   : PolicySanity usb_policy
    usb_refinement    : dispatcher_refines_policy usb_spec usb_policy

UniSchedulerLemmas derives from any bundle:
  uni_scheduler_on            -- concrete Scheduler
  uni_policy_scheduler_on     -- abstract policy Scheduler
  uni_scheduler_on_valid      -- the concrete scheduler is valid
  uni_scheduler_on_refines_policy  -- concrete ⊆ policy
  uni_scheduler_on_schedulable_by_on_intro  -- standard entry point

EDF.v and FIFO.v each gain:
  *_bundle, *_scheduler_on, *_policy_scheduler_on

SchedulableExamples.v adds a bundle-based example showing:
  edf_bundle → uni_scheduler_on → schedulable_by_on

Existing definitions (edf_scheduler, fifo_scheduler, etc.) are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
